mnemonics.title: Thread Purpose
mnemonics.content: THREADS HELP TRAVERSE EFFICIENTLY - TAG=1 FOR CHILD, TAG=0 FOR THREAD (T-1-C, T-0-T)

mnemonics.title: Null Pointers Count
mnemonics.content: NULL POINTERS = NODES + 1 - THINK N+1 (N plus One) - THE EXTRA ONE ALWAYS REMAINS NULL

mnemonics.title: Tag Meaning
mnemonics.content: LTAG/RTAG = 1 MEANS CHILD (1 CHILD), 0 MEANS THREAD (0 CHILD = THREAD)

acronyms.expansion: Left Tag - indicates whether left pointer is child (1) or thread (0)
acronyms.usage: Used to determine how to interpret the left pointer during traversal

acronyms.expansion: Right Tag - indicates whether right pointer is child (1) or thread (0)
acronyms.usage: Used to determine how to interpret the right pointer during traversal

acronyms.expansion: Morris Inorder Traversal - uses temporary threading to achieve O(1) space
acronyms.usage: Alternative to threaded trees that temporarily modifies tree structure

visualTips.visualization: Imagine arrows connecting nodes in traversal order. RIGHT THREAD points forward (successor), LEFT THREAD points backward (predecessor). Draw curved lines between nodes that would otherwise be disconnected.
Think of tag as a traffic signal. TAG=1 means GO (follow to child), TAG=0 means STOP (this pointer is a shortcut/thread). Color code: green for child, yellow for thread.
Draw a tree with n nodes, circle every null pointer in red. Count them. For n=5, you should find 6 red circles. This visual shows the wasted space being reused.

analogies.analogy: Like a train station with signposts. In a regular tree, dead-end paths have no signs. Threaded trees add signposts at dead ends pointing to the next station in sequence, so you never get stuck.

analogies.analization: Tags are like labels on doors. A tag=1 says 'This door leads to a room (child)'. A tag=0 says 'This door is a shortcut to another area (thread)'. You read the label before opening.

analogies.analogy: Like temporarily stringing lights through a dark hallway to find your way, then removing them after. You create temporary paths, use them, then restore everything to original state.
